boundary_binding.action_boundary: May operations activate the production device group?
boundary_binding.allow_rule_assessment: Action may only ALLOW when calibration authority contains every critical term required by policy: device group, site, release condition, activation date, AND calibration-status class.
boundary_binding.controlling_source_fact: STD-GAC-087-P requires calibration-status class in calibration authority before activation; CAL-GAC-087 omits this term.
boundary_binding.escalate_rule_assessment: ESCALATE because calibration authority CAL-GAC-087 lacks explicit calibration-status class term despite WARN-GAC-087 requiring separate calibration status verification for units sharing prefixes with lab devices.
boundary_binding.timing_scope_authority_dependency_check: Timing matches (2026-06-28 before activation), device group GD-4 matches, site P2 matches, release condition RC-12 matches. Missing: calibration-status class.

critical_features_preserved: Source boundary requires exact term matching
Calibration-status class is required term per policy
Device group GD-4, Site P2, RC-12 match confirmed
final_answer: ESCALATE. The action may not proceed. Policy STD-GAC-087-P requires calibration authority to include calibration-status class before activation. While CAL-GAC-087 matches device group GD-4, Site P2, release condition RC-12, and activation date 2026-06-28, it omits the calibration-status class term. WARN-GAC-087 explicitly requires separate calibration-status verification for production devices sharing prefixes with lab units. Source boundary remains OPEN. Exact calibration-status class record required.
open_blockers: CAL-GAC-087 calibration authority does not include calibration-status class term despite policy requirement and warning about shared prefixes requiring separate verification
WARN-GAC-087 explicitly states calibration status must be separately verified for units sharing prefixes
Source boundary cannot close until calibration-status class appears in calibration authority record
